elements.sent-2411-181: Recall \begin{equation}H_{0}^{il}(\Omega)=\left\{ f:\|f\|_{H^{il}}=\underset{|\alpha|\leqslant il}{\sum}\|D^{\alpha}f\|_{L^{2}}<+\infty,\,D^{\alpha}f|_{\partial\Omega}=0 for |\alpha|\leqslant il-1\right\} ,\end{equation} and note that $H_{0 ^{il (\Omega)$ is the completion of $C_{0 ^{\infty (\Omega)$ in $H^{il}$ norm.. Then \begin{equation}\left(\lambda_{k}^{l}\right)^{2} =\underset{{c} M\subseteq H_{0}^{l}(\Omega) dimM=k }{inf}\underset{{c} f\in M \|f\|_{L^{2}}=1 }{sup}\langle\nabla^{l}f,\nabla^{l}f\rangle_{\Omega}^{2} =\underset{{c} M\subseteq C_{0}^{\infty}(\Omega) dimM=k }{inf}\underset{{c} f\in M \|f\|_{L^{2}}=1 }{sup}\langle(-\Delta)^{l}f,f\rangle_{\Omega}^{2} \leqslant\underset{{c} M\subseteq C_{0}^{\infty}(\Omega) dimM=k }{inf}\underset{{c} f\in M \|f\|_{L^{2}}=1 }{sup}\langle(-\Delta)^{l}f,(-\Delta)^{l}f\rangle_{\Omega}\langle f,f\rangle_{\Omega} =\underset{{c} M\subseteq H_{0}^{2l}(\Omega) dimM=k }{inf}\underset{{c} f\in M \|f\|_{L^{2}}=1 }{sup}\langle(-\Delta)^{l}f,(-\Delta)^{l}f\rangle_{\Omega}=\lambda_{k}^{2l}.\end{equation}.}}}}

elements.sent-2411-182: The following numerical experiment illustrates that on the path graph $[0,n]\subseteq\mathbb{Z}$, the ratio of the squares of discrete Dirichlet Laplace eigenvalues and Dirichlet bi-Laplace eigenvalues in Remark \ref{rem: bilaplace estimate (3) approximates the continuous case $(0,n)\subseteq\mathbb{R}$, that is, \begin{equation}\frac{\left(\lambda_{k}^{1}([0,n])\right)^{2}}{\lambda_{k}^{2}([0,n])}\to c_{k}=\frac{\left(\lambda_{k}^{1}((0,1))\right)^{2}}{\lambda_{k}^{2}((0,1))}<1 as n\to+\infty,\end{equation} suggesting that the definition (\ref{eq: main poly-laplace Dirichlet eigenvalue problem ) is reasonable.}}
